query_normalized: predicting the diffusion coefficient of hydrogen in metals from compositional and microstructural descriptors materials science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) because it directly measures the same biological/physical mechanism (hydrogen diffusion in metals) and the dependent variable (diffusion coefficient/behavior) within the user's specific domain (materials science), even though it focuses on amorphous metals and the Arrhenius relation rather than the specific predictive modeling approach requested.
